mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month signals easing financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equity cash flows, which raises S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher when FEDFUNDS is declining versus flat or rising periods, or if the sign of the signal is negative in more than 60% of declining-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, the market prices persistent policy restraint and tighter real rates, which suppresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is above its 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not have worse 20d SPX returns than untriggered observations, or if the interaction term loses sign consistency over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in UNRATE signals weakening labor demand and higher recession probability, which reduces equity risk appetite and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising UNRATE episodes fail to precede negative 20d SPX returns more often than chance, or if the average return after UNRATE increases is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and supply-side momentum, which supports earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO growth does not correspond to higher 60d SPX returns, or if the signal turns negative in a majority of expansionary subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in RSAFS below its recent trend signals weakening consumer demand and softer revenue growth, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth falls below 0 over 2 consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: Reject if the signal fails to predict below-average 20d returns after two-month sales decelerations, or if positive SPX returns dominate triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rebound in HOUST and GDPC1 marks an early-cycle demand recovery in housing and aggregate output, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous housing and GDP improvements do not outperform single-series effects, or if 60d returns are not positive in a majority of joint-rebound episodes.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating in a way that compresses earnings forecasts and increases recession pricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month over month and UNRATE rises by at least 0.1pp in the same release window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS/UNRATE trigger does not produce worse 5d returns than either series alone, or if triggered episodes show no negative edge versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During a restrictive policy regime, stable CPIAUCSL combined with an unchanged or lower FEDFUNDS rate signals that real rate pressure is easing, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20d returns do not concentrate in periods of flat or falling FEDFUNDS with stable inflation, or if the interaction loses predictive value in tightening regimes.
